panels.description: Wide shot of a dimly lit, dusty underground tunnel. The narrator and the artilleryman are digging with spades. The atmosphere is tense and claustrophobic. The narrator looks skeptical while working, sweat on his brow, shadows emphasizing his furrowed expression.

bubbles.text: Why this long tunnel? We could just go down a manhole.

panels.description: Medium shot of the artilleryman stopping his work, dropping his spade to the dirt. He looks up at the narrator with an evasive, weary look. The tunnel light catches the dust motes around them.

bubbles.text: Let us knock off a bit. Time we reconnoitred from the roof.

panels.description: Medium close-up split panel. Left side shows the narrator narrowing his eyes, suspecting the artilleryman. Right side shows the artilleryman looking away, sweating nervously. High contrast, noir-style lighting.

bubbles.text: Why were you walking the common instead of being here?
Taking the air. It's safer by night.

panels.description: Wide shot, exterior rooftop. The two men are peering over a brick parapet. Below them, the river is a vivid, horrifying bubbly mass of red alien weed. The sky is hazy and ominous.
Close-up on the artilleryman's face, wide-eyed, telling the story of the carnage in London. The background is slightly blurred, focusing on his frantic, terrified expression.

bubbles.text: They were dancing under the electric lights, and the machine came down the road... ablaze with people who couldn't run.
